mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (below 0.25%) signals persistent monetary accommodation that lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains low.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or positive-return hit rate during low-rate periods is not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points from its prior 12-month trough (a Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals an incipient labor market downturn that precedes broad earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days once triggered.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional returns using a t-test at p > 0.05 across the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) accelerates while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains flat, the combination indicates strengthening consumer demand without offsetting monetary tightening, jointly producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; neither series alone is sufficient because rising RSAFS with rising FEDFUNDS reflects tightening that offsets the demand boost.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS month-over-month change <= 0.02pp
failure_condition: Joint-condition instances show 5-day forward positive-return hit rate not exceeding the unconditional base rate by more than 2 percentage points, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the trend persists, independent of any triggering threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips negative for more than one full year.

mechanism_narrative: The relationship between CPI inflation trend and forward equity returns is regime-dependent: during a monetary easing cycle (falling FEDFUNDS), rising CPIAUCSL is interpreted as reflation supporting risk assets and predicts positive returns, whereas during a tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS) the same CPI rise predicts negative returns due to rate-hike fears, over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle vs tightening cycle divergence
failure_condition: The sign of the CPI-return relationship does not flip between easing and tightening subsamples, or Rank IC in the easing subsample remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals continued labor market expansion and consumer income growth, producing a short-lived positive S&P 500 forward return over the next 5 trading days following each payroll print, reflecting immediate market reaction to labor strength.
failure_condition: 5-day forward positive-return hit rate following positive PAYEMS growth does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% base rate by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month average signals weakening forward-looking consumer and construction demand, a historically reliable leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days once triggered.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, the joint deterioration in supply-side output and labor slack signals an emerging contraction that a single indicator alone would miss, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=106.1878 and UNRATE=5.5 as of early 2015
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show negative-return hit rate not exceeding 45%, or the interaction coefficient in a walk-forward logistic regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
